summary: Live-applied ADR 0240 on the interactive ops portal by deploying the rebased Apache ECharts portal bundle, restoring exact branch-matching runtime files on docker-runtime-lv3, rebuilding the ops-portal container, and re-verifying the guest-local and public operator entrypoints after the scoped converge runner was interrupted locally.

verification.observed: `python3 -m py_compile scripts/ops_portal/app.py scripts/ops_portal/runtime_assurance.py`, `make syntax-check-ops-portal`, `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema --with-requirements requirements/ops-portal.txt python -m pytest -q tests/test_interactive_ops_portal.py tests/test_runtime_assurance_scoreboard.py tests/test_ops_portal.py` (`19 passed in 55.09s`), `make validate-data-models`, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` all passed from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0240-live-apply`.

Rebased `make converge-ops-portal` runs from the worktree validated the `ops_portal_runtime` overlay-directory fix and surfaced a transient `DOCKER-FORWARD` chain recheck race in `docker_runtime`, but later workstation-scoped replays were interrupted locally with signal `15` before the guest-local portal hashes changed.

verification.check: Manual payload mirror and rebuild fallback
verification.observed: Because the interrupted converges left stale portal sources on `docker-runtime-lv3`, the exact branch payload from `scripts/ops_portal`, `scripts/search_fabric`, `scripts/publication_contract.py`, `requirements/ops-portal.txt`, `config/`, `changelog.md`, and `receipts/` was mirrored directly to `/opt/ops-portal/service` and `/opt/ops-portal/data`, then rebuilt with `cd /opt/ops-portal && sudo docker compose up -d --build --remove-orphans`.

Over the Proxmox jump path to `ops@10.10.10.20`, `sha256sum` for `/opt/ops-portal/service/ops_portal/app.py`, `runtime_assurance.py`, `templates/partials/overview.html`, `static/portal.css`, `static/portal.js`, and `/opt/ops-portal/service/requirements.txt` matched the branch-local hashes exactly; `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8092/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}`; and `docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}" | grep -E "^(NAMES|ops-portal)"` showed the rebuilt `ops-portal` container up.

`curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8092/` rendered the committed ECharts `<script src="https://unpkg.com/echarts@5.6.0/dist/echarts.min.js"></script>` and same-origin `/static/portal.js` bundle; `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8092/static/portal.js | grep -E "echarts|data-echart-target"` returned the chart bootstrap code; and `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8092/partials/overview | grep -E "Runtime Assurance|data-echart-target"` confirmed the runtime-assurance block plus the mounted chart targets.

`curl -k -I https://ops.example.com` returned `302` to `https://ops.example.com/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://ops.example.com/` with the expected hardening headers and a CSP that still allows `https://unpkg.com` for the committed ECharts runtime source.

notes: The live platform now serves the exact branch-tip portal hashes from commit `5783e707df213eb16994b6da1e3e1b8f930bcd5a`, but the final merged-main replay still needs to be rerun from `main` before updating protected release surfaces.

This receipt records a manual guest-side payload mirror because the local scoped converge runner was interrupted repeatedly from this workstation session; the runbook now documents the same hash-compare and rebuild fallback.
Canonical repo-version bumps, changelog release notes, README integrated truth, and `versions/stack.yaml` updates still belong to the final merge-to-main step.
